revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Joyama Kofun Group]][[ja:城山古墳群 (香取市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Joyama Kofun Group]][[ja:城山古墳群 (香取市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Joyama Kofun Group]][[ja:城山古墳群 (香取市)]] [[ja:城山古墳群 (香取市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.859027777777776|N|140.58988888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} [[File:Joyama Kofun Group (Katori)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|{{ill|Joyama Tomb No.1 (Katori)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joyama Tomb No.1 (Katori)|qq=}} Relocated Stone Chamber}}]] {{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=51|lat_sec=32.45|lon_deg=140|lon_min=35|lon_sec=23.55|label={{ill|Joyama Kofun Group|ja|城山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joyama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Joyama Kofun Group}}}} '''Joyama Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Joyama Kofun Group|ja|城山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joyama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is an ancient cluster of Kofuns (kofun) located in Komigawa, {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|qid=Q858357|lt=Katori City|1=Katori, Chiba}}, Chiba Prefecture. One of the mounds (Tomb No.4) has been designated as a historic site by Katori City, and artifacts excavated from Tomb No.1 have been designated as a tangible cultural property by Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun group was constructed on a plateau (commonly known as "Joyama") at an elevation of approximately 42 meters on the southern bank of the Tone River (formerly the {{ill|Kinugawa River|en|Kinugawa River|qid=Q1042040|lt=Kinugawa|1=Kinugawa River}}) in its lower reaches, and on the left bank of its tributary, the Kurobe River. The group consists of over 20 mounds, including 8 keyhole-shaped mounds ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}), 2 square mounds ({{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}), 11 round mounds ({{ill|Enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}), and 7 mounds of unknown shape{{Sfn|Joyama Kofun Group (Continuation of Kofun)|2002}}. Since 1963 (Shōwa 38), archaeological excavations have been carried out in connection with school construction and park development{{Sfn|Joyama Kofun Group (Continuation of Kofun)|2002}}. The largest mound is '''{{ill|Joyama Tomb No.1 (Katori)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joyama Tomb No.1 (Katori)|qq=}}''' – a keyhole-shaped mound with a mound length of 68 meters. Excavations of Tomb No.1 have yielded numerous grave goods, including a triangular-rimmed mirror depicting three deities and five mythical beasts, as well as weapons, armaments, and decorative items. This makes it one of the most significant late-period Kofuns in the Kantō region. Many kofun were built around Tomb No.1 as subordinate mounds; notably, the high proportion of keyhole-shaped mounds, the frequent use of horizontal tunnel-type stone chambers, and the prevalence of haniwa rows are remarkable features{{Sfn|Joyama Kofun Group (Chiba Prefecture History, Materials Edition Archaeology 2)|2003}}. In particular, Tomb No.4, estimated to have been built around the same time as Tomb No.1 but at roughly half its size, offers important insights into the social hierarchy of the interred clan[https://www.city.katori.lg.jp/smph/culture_sport/bunkazai/isan/isan_vol041-050.html#cmsVol050 Joyama Tomb No.4] (Katori City Website). The construction period is estimated to be from the later Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, roughly the mid-6th to early 7th centuries{{Sfn|Joyama Kofun Group (Chiba Prefecture History, Materials Edition Archaeology 2)|2003}}. In the lower reaches of the Tone River, this is one of the most prominent late-period kofun groups, and along with nearby sites such as the {{ill|Kiuchi Haiji Site|ja|木内廃寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiuchi Haiji Site|1=Kinouchi Temple Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q132737551}} (Kiuchi ruins, Katori City) and the Kiyomizu Tiled Kiln (located in Mushiba, Katori City), it has been linked to the "Kuni-zo Motonokuni" mentioned in the "Kuni-zo Honki" of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Kujiki|1=Kujiki}}{{Sfn|Joyama Kofun Group (Chiba Prefecture History, Materials Edition Archaeology 2)|2003}}. Tomb No.4 was designated as a historic site by Maruya-machi (currently designated by Katori City) in 1973 (Shōwa 48), and the artifacts from Tomb No.1 were designated as a tangible cultural property by Chiba Prefecture in 1969 (Shōwa 44). Today, part of the kofun group is preserved within Joyama Park. == Archaeological History == * Before World War II, the Joyama Kofun Group was recorded in the "Katori Gunshi" and the "Chitei Tankenki"{{Sfn|Joyama Kofun Group (Chiba Prefecture History, Materials Edition Archaeology 2)|2003}}. * In November 1963 (Shōwa 38), an investigation of Tomb No.1 was conducted due to the relocation of the prefectural Komigawa High School (with a report published by Risshō University in 1978). * In 1964 (Shōwa 39), a group of Sarcophaguss from Tomb No.20 was investigated during the leveling of the site for the high school (by Sugiyama Shinsaku and others). * In 1965 (Shōwa 40), survey work was conducted on Tomb No.2, and excavation work was carried out on Tomb Nos. 5 and 7-1 (with a report published by Risshō University in 1978). * In February 1966 (Shōwa 41), an excavation of Tomb No.6 was performed during the construction of a new building for Komigawa Junior High School (by Waseda University). * On April 18, 1969 (Shōwa 44), the artifacts excavated from Tomb No.1 were designated as tangible cultural properties by Chiba Prefecture. * In May 1970 (Shōwa 45), an excavation of Tomb No.9 was carried out (by Waseda University). * On April 23, 1973 (Shōwa 48), Tomb No.4 was designated as a historic site by Katori City. * In October 1985 (Shōwa 60), a survey and confirmation of the peripheral moats of Tomb No.13 were conducted in advance of renovations at Seifūsō (by the Komigawa Town Board of Education). * In March 1987 (Shōwa 62), a survey of Tomb No.14 was carried out as part of the expansion of Joyama Park (by the Komigawa Town Board of Education). * In May 1991 (Heisei 3), an excavation of Tomb No.14 was conducted during the park expansion (by the Katori City Cultural Properties Center). * In 1992 (Heisei 4), a survey and excavation of Tomb No.4 were undertaken (by the Katori City Cultural Properties Center, with a report published in 1993). * In 1996 (Heisei 8), an excavation of Tomb No.3 was conducted as part of a leveling project (by the Katori City Cultural Properties Center, with a report published in 1997). == List == {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ List of the Joyama Kofun Group{{Sfn|Joyama Kofun Group (Continuation of Kofun)|2002}}{{Sfn|Joyama Kofun Group (Chiba Prefecture History, Materials Edition Archaeology 2)|2003}} ! Kofun Name ! Shape ! Scale ! Burial Facility ! Grave Goods ! Period of Construction ! Remarks |- | '''{{ill|Joyama Tomb No.1 (Katori)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joyama Tomb No.1 (Katori)|qq=}}''' || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} || Mound length: 68 m || {{ill|Corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(with a [[Wooden Coffin|wooden coffin]] inside) || Numerous subsidiary burial goods; haniwa || Mid-6th century || Excavated in 1963; the stone chamber was relocated and preserved within Joyama Park; artifacts are designated as a tangible cultural property of Chiba Prefecture. |- | '''Tomb No.2'''
(traditionally the Tomb of Kōsei-ō) || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} || Mound length: 45 m || (Presumed) {{ill|Corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}|| || || Survey conducted in 1965. |- | '''Tomb No.3'''
(traditionally Himezuka) || {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Side length: 19 m || Possibly two box-type [[Sarcophaguss]]|| At mound top: 3 straight-edged blades, a knife, an iron arrowhead, 4 Sue pottery vessels; at mound base: 2 straight-edged blades, 2 Sue pottery vessels || Early 7th century || Excavated in 1996. |- | '''Tomb No.4''' || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} || Mound length: 34 m || || Haniwa || Mid-6th century || Surveyed and excavated in 1992; designated as a historic site by Katori City. |- | '''Tomb No.5''' || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} || Mound length: 51 m || Possibly a direct burial in a [[Wooden Coffin|wooden coffin]]|| Copper pendants; iron fragments; molded objects made of talc; Sue pottery; earthenware; haniwa || Early to mid-6th century || Excavated in 1965. |- | '''Tomb No.6''' || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} || Mound length: 42 m || {{ill|Corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}|| Iron arrowhead fragments; iron nails; whetstones || 7th century || Survey conducted in 1966. |- | '''Tomb No.7-1''' || Possibly an {{ill|Enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}? || || Underground box-type Sarcophagus || Human bones; iron nails; Sue pottery || Mid-7th century || Excavated in 1965. |- | '''Tomb No.7-2''' || {{ill|Enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || || || || || |- | '''Tomb No.8''' || {{ill|Enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Diameter: 5 m || || || || |- | '''Tomb No.9''' || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} || Mound length: 49 m || {{ill|Corridor-style burial chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style burial chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}|| Sue pottery || || Excavated in 1970. |- | '''Tomb No.10-1''' || {{ill|Enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Diameter: 15 m || || || || |- | '''Tomb No.10-2''' || {{ill|Enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Diameter: 10 m || || || || |- | '''Tomb No.11''' || {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Side length: 10 m || || || After the 7th century || |- | '''Tomb No.12''' || {{ill|Enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || || || || || (No longer exists) |- | '''Tomb No.13''' || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} || Mound length: 40 m || Possibly a horizontal tunnel-type stone chamber? || Haniwa || Mid-6th century || Surveyed and confirmed in 1985. |- | '''Tomb No.14''' || {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} || Mound length: 30.5 m || Underground box-type [[Sekkan|Sarcophagus]]|| Iron knife fragments; Sue pottery; earthenware; haniwa || Mid-6th century || Levelled during the construction of Komigawa Castle; confirmed in 1987 and excavated in 1991. |- | '''Tomb No.15''' || {{ill|Enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || || || || || |- | '''Tomb No.16''' || {{ill|Enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || || || || || |- | '''Tomb No.17''' || {{ill|Enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || || || || || |- | '''Tomb No.18''' || {{ill|Enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || || || || || |- | '''Tomb No.19''' || {{ill|Enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || || || || || (No longer exists) |- | '''Tomb No.20''' || An irregular kofun || || 7 underground [[Sarcophaguss]]|| Straight-edged blades; knife fragments || Late 6th to 7th century || Surveyed in 1964. |} File:城山1号墳 (香取市) 出土 三角縁三神五獣鏡 (複製).JPG|Replicated Triangular-Rimmed Mirror of Three Deities and Five Beasts from Tomb No.1 (displayed at the Katori City Cultural Properties Preservation Museum; similar images exist) File:城山1号墳 (香取市) 出土 装飾付大刀.JPG|Decorated Great Sword from Tomb No.1 File:城山4号墳 (香取市) 出土 形象埴輪.JPG|Figurative Haniwa from Tomb No.4 File:城山4号墳 (香取市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa from Tomb No.4 File:城山5号墳 (香取市) 出土品.JPG|burial goods from Tomb No.5 File:城山13号墳 (香取市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa from Tomb No.13 == Cultural Properties == === Chiba Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Movable Cultural Property: ** Artifacts from {{ill|Joyama Tomb No.1 (Katori)|ja|城山第一号古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joyama Tomb No.1 (Katori)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Managed by the Katori City Cultural Properties Preservation Museum. Designated on April 18, 1969 (Shōwa 44)[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p181-010.html Artifacts from Joyama Tomb No.1] (Chiba Prefectural Board of Education “Chiba Cultural Properties”). === Katori City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ** Tomb No.4 of the Joyama Kofun Group – Designated on April 23, 1973 (Shōwa 48). == Related Facilities == * Katori City Cultural Properties Preservation Museum (located in Hanegawa, within the Komigawa Citizen Center Ibuki-kan, Katori City) – where the artifacts from the Joyama Kofun Group are preserved and exhibited. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == {{small|(Literature used in the preparation of this article)}} * Explanation board for Joyama Tomb No.1 (installed by the Katori City Board of Education, 2020) * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Joyama Tomb No.1|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 12: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvid|Joyama Tomb No.1 (Heibonsha)|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|year=1989|chapter=Joyama Tomb No.1|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=東京堂出版|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Joyama Tomb No.1 (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=Kōhō Hirano|year=2002|chapter=Joyama Kofun Group|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]] (Continued)|publisher=東京堂出版|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Joyama Kofun Group (Continued)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|author=|year=2003|chapter=Joyama Kofun Group|title=Chiba Prefecture History: Materials Edition Archaeology 2 (Ken-shi Series 10)|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Joyama Kofun Group (Chiba Prefecture History, Materials Edition Archaeology 2)|2003}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not used in the preparation of this article)}} * Reports: ** {{Cite book|lang=ja|author=|year=1965|chapter=|title=Investigation of the Stone Coffin Group of the Joyama Kofun in Komigawa Town, Chiba Prefecture (Report, Risshō University Museum Studies, Vol.2)|publisher=Risshō University Faculty of Literature Museum Studies|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Cite book|lang=ja|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=Joyama Tomb No.1 Keyhole-shaped Kofun|publisher=Komigawa Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Cite book|lang=ja|author=|year=1993|chapter=|title=Joyama Tomb No.4 (Investigation Report, Katori City Cultural Properties Center, Vol.19)|publisher=Katori City Cultural Properties Center|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Cite book|lang=ja|author=|year=1997|chapter=|title=Joyama Tomb No.3 (Investigation Report, Katori City Cultural Properties Center, Vol.52)|publisher=Katori City Cultural Properties Center|isbn=|ref=}} * Others: ** {{Cite journal|lang=ja|author=Ichimō Isao and Ōta Tazunori|title=Investigation of the Stone Coffin Group Discovered at Komigawa, Katori Town, Chiba Prefecture and Joyama Tomb No.6|date=December 1974|journal=古代|publisher=Waseda University Archaeological Society|pages=26–36|ref=}} ** {{Cite journal|lang=ja|author=Asami Kondō|title=Special Stone Chamber 2 at Joyama Tomb No.6 and its Surroundings in the Bōsō Peninsula|date=March 31, 1977|journal=史館|publisher=史館同人|pages=1–6|ref=}} ** {{Cite journal|lang=ja|author=Yūko Hashimoto|title=Statistical Analysis of the Classification of Figure Haniwa: A Case Study of Joyama Tomb No.1 in Chiba Prefecture|date=May 15, 1995|journal=埴輪研究会誌|publisher=埴輪研究会|pages=37–59|ref=}} ** {{Cite journal|lang=ja|author=Masayoshi Jōkurau|title=Figure Haniwa Excavated from Joyama Tomb No.5 in Chiba Prefecture|date=May 2007|journal=埴輪研究会誌|publisher=埴輪研究会|pages=149–158|ref=}} ** {{Cite journal|lang=ja|author=Inuuki Tsutomu|title=A Brief Discussion on the Row of Haniwa at Joyama Tomb No.1: Focusing on the Rear Haniwa Row|date=May 29, 2011|journal=埴輪研究会誌|publisher=埴輪研究会|pages=79–92|ref=}} ** {{Cite journal|lang=ja|author=Asami Kondō|title=On the Haniwa Excavated from Joyama Tomb No.4 in Katori City, Chiba Prefecture|date=May 2014|journal=埴輪研究会誌|publisher=埴輪研究会|pages=163–179|ref=}} ** {{Cite journal|lang=ja|author=Inuuki Tsutomu|title=Excavation Record Photographs of Joyama Tomb No.1 (1) – Basic Materials of the Joyama Kofun Group in Katori City, Part 1 (Section 1)|date=February 2016|journal=大阪大谷大学紀要|publisher=大阪大谷大学志学会|pages=1–46|ref=}} ** {{Cite journal|lang=ja|author=Inuuki Tsutomu|title=Excavation Record Photographs of Joyama Tomb No.5 (1) – Basic Materials of the Joyama Kofun Group in Katori City, Part 2 (Section 1)|date=February 2017|journal=大阪大谷大学紀要|publisher=大阪大谷大学志学会|pages=1–32|ref=}} ** {{Cite journal|lang=ja|author=Inuuki Tsutomu|title=Excavation Record Photographs of Joyama Tomb No.1 (2) – Basic Materials of the Joyama Kofun Group in Katori City, Part 1 (Section 2)|date=February 2018|journal=大阪大谷大学紀要|publisher=大阪大谷大学志学会|pages=13–54|ref=}} ** {{Cite journal|lang=ja|author=Inuuki Tsutomu|title=Excavation Record Photographs of Joyama Tomb No.1 (3) – Basic Materials of the Joyama Kofun Group in Katori City, Part 1 (Section 3)|date=March 2019|journal=志學臺考古|publisher=大阪大谷大学歴史文化学科|pages=46–21|ref=}} == Related
